title: CEO Commentary on Shares & Deposits: As of March 31, 2026
content:
Total member shares declined by $954 thousand as of March 31, 2026, representing a -6.0% decrease from the prior period. This contraction reflects an overall reduction in our funding base.

Regular Shares remain the largest funding component at 52.0% of total shares, totaling $8,178 thousand ($9 million). Share Drafts account for $3,471 thousand (22.1%), while Share Certificates totaled $2,978 thousand (18.9%). All Other Shares represented a meaningful portion at 6.1%, continuing to represent a significant slice of the portfolio. IRA/Keogh Accounts totalled $150 thousand (1.0%) of the funding mix.

Most core deposit categories declined from the previous period, particularly Regular Shares (-17.9%), Share Drafts (-11.7%), and All Other Shares (+6.6%).

Overall, Northern Valley's deposit structure remains well diversified and predominantly composed of core member funding. Management continues to focus on retaining core member relationships, maintaining deposit competitiveness, and supporting long-term funding stability.
